methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Anthrogen is a Seed-stage biotech company founded by Ankit Singhal that has raised $4.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a sector universe of 11 peers—3 public companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private firms including Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics—though the narrow peer set limits methodological options.

Only the Stage-Progression method is applicable here. Using total capital raised ($4.5M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to Series A yields an implied valuation of $11.25M. Peer-Median analysis is inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow biotech filter (fewer than 3 datapoints). Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be deployed because Anthrogen's revenue is not disclosed; while the public Biotech & Health median trades at 5.8x sales across the three public comps, this multiple cannot be applied without the target's top-line figure.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $10M (low) to $13M (high), with a median of $11M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodology. Key uncertainties include the absence of disclosed revenue, limiting the applicability of market-based multiples, and the sparsity of directly comparable Seed-stage private peers, which constrains calibration against stage-specific risk and growth profiles.
